A small paintbrush then applies a dark liquid to the clay. Next, the hands pour pink ceramic powder from a clear glass jar into the ceramic palette, which already features a green leaf design. The piano music continues as the hands open a rustic wooden drawer, revealing an organized collection of ceramic tools, including loop tools and a metal scraper, which are then placed on a wooden board with distinct clinking and scraping sounds. The well-lit, warm-toned wooden workspace, consistently organized and aesthetically pleasing, showcases various ceramic pieces and tools in the background. The hands then handle sealed, translucent plastic bags containing pink, green, and blue ceramic powders, producing soft rustling and crinkling sounds as they are placed on a gray cutting mat. The powders are subsequently poured one by one into clear glass jars—pink first, then green, and finally blue—with gentle pouring sounds accompanying each transfer. On-screen text appears, stating, 'In this video, I wanted to show you the steps of my ceramic products that I made for myself,' and later, 'I will not be including detailed technical information because I am not a professional in this field.'
